content.content:
1	"""
2	Process Registry -- In-memory registry for managed background processes.
3	
4	Tracks processes spawned via terminal(background=true), providing:
5	  - Output buffering (rolling 200KB window)
6	  - Status polling and log retrieval
7	  - Blocking wait with interrupt support
8	  - Process killing
9	  - Crash recovery via JSON checkpoint file
10	  - Session-scoped tracking for gateway reset protection
11	
12	Background processes execute THROUGH the environment interface -- nothing
13	runs on the host machine unless TERMINAL_ENV=local. For Docker, Singularity,
14	Modal, Daytona, and SSH backends, the command runs inside the sandbox.
15	
16	Usage:
17	    from tools.process_registry import process_registry
18	
19	    # Spawn a background process (called from terminal_tool)
20	    session = process_registry.spawn(env, "pytest -v", task_id="task_123")
21	
22	    # Poll for status
23	    result = process_registry.poll(session.id)
24	
25	    # Block until done
26	    result = process_registry.wait(session.id, timeout=300)
27	
28	    # Kill it
29	    process_registry.kill(session.id)
30	"""
31	
32	import json
33	import logging
34	import os
35	import platform
36	import shlex
37	import signal
38	import subprocess
39	import threading
40	import time
41	import uuid
42	
43	_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"
44	from tools.environments.local import _find_shell, _sanitize_subprocess_env
45	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
46	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
47	
48	from hermes_cli.config import get_hermes_home
49	
50	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
51	
52	
53	# Checkpoint file for crash recovery (gateway only)
54	CHECKPOINT_PATH = get_hermes_home() / "processes.json"
55	
56	# Limits
57	MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS = 200_000      # 200KB rolling output buffer
58	FINISHED_TTL_SECONDS = 1800     # Keep finished processes for 30 minutes
59	MAX_PROCESSES = 64              # Max concurrent tracked processes (LRU pruning)
60	
61	
62	@dataclass
63	class ProcessSession:
64	    """A tracked background process with output buffering."""
65	    id: str                                     # Unique session ID ("proc_xxxxxxxxxxxx")
66	    command: str                                 # Original command string
67	    task_id: str = ""                           # Task/sandbox isolation key
68	    session_key: str = ""                       # Gateway session key (for reset protection)
69	    pid: Optional[int] = None                   # OS process ID
70	    process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None  # Popen handle (local only)
71	    env_ref: Any = None                         # Reference to the environment object
72	    cwd: Optional[str] = None                   # Working directory
73	    started_at: float = 0.0                     # time.time() of spawn
74	    exited: bool = False                        # Whether the process has finished
75	    exit_code: Optional[int] = None             # Exit code (None if still running)
76	    output_buffer: str = ""                     # Rolling output (last MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS)
77	    max_output_chars: int = MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS
78	    detached: bool = False                      # True if recovered from crash (no pipe)
79	    # Watcher/notification metadata (persisted for crash recovery)
80	    watcher_platform: str = ""
81	    watcher_chat_id: str = ""
82	    watcher_thread_id: str = ""
83	    watcher_interval: int = 0                   # 0 = no watcher configured
84	    _lock: threading.Lock = field(default_factory=threading.Lock)
85	    _reader_thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = field(default=None, repr=False)
86	    _pty: Any = field(default=None, repr=False)  # ptyprocess handle (when use_pty=True)
87	
88	
89	class ProcessRegistry:
90	    """
91	    In-memory registry of running and finished background processes.
92	
93	    Thread-safe. Accessed from:
94	      - Executor threads (terminal_tool, process tool handlers)
95	      - Gateway asyncio loop (watcher tasks, session reset checks)
96	      - Cleanup thread (sandbox reaping coordination)
97	    """
98	
99	    _SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS = (
100	        "bash: cannot set terminal process group",
101	        "bash: no job control in this shell",
102	        "no job control in this shell",
103	        "cannot set terminal process group",
104	        "tcsetattr: Inappropriate ioctl for device",
105	    )
106	
107	    def __init__(self):
108	        self._running: Dict[str, ProcessSession] = {}
109	        self._finished: Dict[str, ProcessSession] = {}
110	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
111	
112	        # Side-channel for check_interval watchers (gateway reads after agent run)
113	        self.pending_watchers: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
114	
115	    @staticmethod
116	    def _clean_shell_noise(text: str) -> str:
117	        """Strip shell startup warnings from the beginning of output."""
118	        lines = text.split("\n")
119	        while lines and any(noise in lines[0] for noise in ProcessRegistry._SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS):
120	            lines.pop(0)
121	        return "\n".join(lines)
122	
123	    # ----- Spawn -----
124	
125	    def spawn_local(
126	        self,
127	        command: str,
128	        cwd: str = None,
129	        task_id: str = "",
130	        session_key: str = "",
131	        env_vars: dict = None,
132	        use_pty: bool = False,
133	    ) -> ProcessSession:
134	        """
135	        Spawn a background process locally.
136	
137	        Only for TERMINAL_ENV=local. Other backends use spawn_via_env().
138	
139	        Args:
140	            use_pty: If True, use a pseudo-terminal via ptyprocess for interactive
141	                     CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL). Falls back to
142	                     subprocess.Popen if ptyprocess is not installed.
143	        """
144	        session = ProcessSession(
145	            id=f"proc_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}",
146	            command=command,
147	            task_id=task_id,
148	            session_key=session_key,
149	            cwd=cwd or os.getcwd(),
150	            started_at=time.time(),
151	        )
152	
153	        if use_pty:
154	            # Try PTY mode for interactive CLI tools
155	            try:
156	                if _IS_WINDOWS:
157	                    from winpty import PtyProcess as _PtyProcessCls
158	                else:
159	                    from ptyprocess import PtyProcess as _PtyProcessCls
160	                user_shell = _find_shell()
161	                pty_env = _sanitize_subprocess_env(os.environ, env_vars)
162	                pty_env["PYTHONUNBUFFERED"] = "1"
163	                pty_proc = _PtyProcessCls.spawn(
164	                    [user_shell, "-lic", command],
165	                    cwd=session.cwd,
166	                    env=pty_env,
167	                    dimensions=(30, 120),
168	                )
169	                session.pid = pty_proc.pid
170	                # Store the pty handle on the session for read/write
171	                session._pty = pty_proc
172	
173	                # PTY reader thread
174	                reader = threading.Thread(
175	                    target=self._pty_reader_loop,
176	                    args=(session,),
177	                    daemon=True,
178	                    name=f"proc-pty-reader-{session.id}",
179	                )
180	                session._reader_thread = reader
181	                reader.start()
182	
183	                with self._lock:
184	                    self._prune_if_needed()
185	                    self._running[session.id] = session
186	
187	                self._write_checkpoint()
188	                return session
189	
190	            except ImportError:
191	                logger.warning("ptyprocess not installed, falling back to pipe mode")
192	            except Exception as e:
193	                logger.warning("PTY spawn failed (%s), falling back to pipe mode", e)
194	
195	        # Standard Popen path (non-PTY or PTY fallback)
196	        # Use the user's login shell for consistency with LocalEnvironment --
197	        # ensures rc files are sourced and user tools are available.
198	        user_shell = _find_shell()
199	        # Force unbuffered output for Python scripts so progress is visible
200	        # during background execution (libraries like tqdm/datasets buffer when
201	        # stdout is a pipe, hiding output from process(action="poll")).
202	        bg_env = _sanitize_subprocess_env(os.environ, env_vars)
203	        bg_env["PYTHONUNBUFFERED"] = "1"
204	        proc = subprocess.Popen(
205	            [user_shell, "-lic", command],
206	            text=True,
207	            cwd=session.cwd,
208	            env=bg_env,
209	            encoding="utf-8",
210	            errors="replace",
211	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
212	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
213	            stdin=subprocess.PIPE,
214	            preexec_fn=None if _IS_WINDOWS else os.setsid,
215	        )
216	
217	        session.process = proc
218	        session.pid = proc.pid
219	
220	        # Start output reader thread
221	        reader = threading.Thread(
222	            target=self._reader_loop,
223	            args=(session,),
224	            daemon=True,
225	            name=f"proc-reader-{session.id}",
226	        )
227	        session._reader_thread = reader
228	        reader.start()
229	
230	        with self._lock:
231	            self._prune_if_needed()
232	            self._running[session.id] = session
233	
234	        self._write_checkpoint()
235	        return session
236	
237	    def spawn_via_env(
238	        self,
239	        env: Any,
240	        command: str,
241	        cwd: str = None,
242	        task_id: str = "",
243	        session_key: str = "",
244	        timeout: int = 10,
245	    ) -> ProcessSession:
246	        """
247	        Spawn a background process through a non-local environment backend.
248	
249	        For Docker/Singularity/Modal/Daytona/SSH: runs the command inside the sandbox
250	        using the environment's execute() interface. We wrap the command to
251	        capture the in-sandbox PID and redirect output to a log file inside
252	        the sandbox, then poll the log via subsequent execute() calls.
253	
254	        This is less capable than local spawn (no live stdout pipe, no stdin),
255	        but it ensures the command runs in the correct sandbox context.
256	        """
257	        session = ProcessSession(
258	            id=f"proc_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}",
259	            command=command,
260	            task_id=task_id,
261	            session_key=session_key,
262	            cwd=cwd,
263	            started_at=time.time(),
264	            env_ref=env,
265	        )
266	
267	        # Run the command in the sandbox with output capture
268	        log_path = f"/tmp/hermes_bg_{session.id}.log"
269	        pid_path = f"/tmp/hermes_bg_{session.id}.pid"
270	        quoted_command = shlex.quote(command)
271	        bg_command = (
272	            f"nohup bash -c {quoted_command} > {log_path} 2>&1 & "
273	            f"echo $! > {pid_path} && cat {pid_path}"
274	        )
275	
276	        try:
277	            result = env.execute(bg_command, timeout=timeout)
278	            output = result.get("output", "").strip()
279	            # Try to extract the PID from the output
280	            for line in output.splitlines():
281	                line = line.strip()
282	                if line.isdigit():
283	                    session.pid = int(line)
284	                    break
285	        except Exception as e:
286	            session.exited = True
287	            session.exit_code = -1
288	            session.output_buffer = f"Failed to start: {e}"
289	
290	        if not session.exited:
291	            # Start a poller thread that periodically reads the log file
292	            reader = threading.Thread(
293	                target=self._env_poller_loop,
294	                args=(session, env, log_path, pid_path),
295	                daemon=True,
296	                name=f"proc-poller-{session.id}",
297	            )
298	            session._reader_thread = reader
299	            reader.start()
300	
301	        with self._lock:
302	            self._prune_if_needed()
303	            self._running[session.id] = session
304	
305	        self._write_checkpoint()
306	        return session
307	
308	    # ----- Reader / Poller Threads -----
309	
310	    def _reader_loop(self, session: ProcessSession):
311	        """Background thread: read stdout from a local Popen process."""
312	        first_chunk = True
313	        try:
314	            while True:
315	                chunk = session.process.stdout.read(4096)
316	                if not chunk:
317	                    break
318	                if first_chunk:
319	                    chunk = self._clean_shell_noise(chunk)
320	                    first_chunk = False
321	                with session._lock:
322	                    session.output_buffer += chunk
323	                    if len(session.output_buffer) > session.max_output_chars:
324	                        session.output_buffer = session.output_buffer[-session.max_output_chars:]
325	        except Exception as e:
326	            logger.debug("Process stdout reader ended: %s", e)
327	
328	        # Process exited
329	        try:
330	            session.process.wait(timeout=5)
331	        except Exception as e:
332	            logger.debug("Process wait timed out or failed: %s", e)
333	        session.exited = True
334	        session.exit_code = session.process.returncode
335	        self._move_to_finished(session)
336	
337	    def _env_poller_loop(
338	        self, session: ProcessSession, env: Any, log_path: str, pid_path: str
339	    ):
340	        """Background thread: poll a sandbox log file for non-local backends."""
341	        while not session.exited:
342	            time.sleep(2)  # Poll every 2 seconds
343	            try:
344	                # Read new output from the log file
345	                result = env.execute(f"cat {log_path} 2>/dev/null", timeout=10)
346	                new_output = result.get("output", "")
347	                if new_output:
348	                    with session._lock:
349	                        session.output_buffer = new_output
350	                        if len(session.output_buffer) > session.max_output_chars:
351	                            session.output_buffer = session.output_buffer[-session.max_output_chars:]
352	
353	                # Check if process is still running
354	                check = env.execute(
355	                    f"kill -0 $(cat {pid_path} 2>/dev/null) 2>/dev/null; echo $?",
356	                    timeout=5,
357	                )
358	                check_output = check.get("output", "").strip()
359	                if check_output and check_output.splitlines()[-1].strip() != "0":
360	                    # Process has exited -- get exit code
361	                    exit_result = env.execute(
362	                        f"wait $(cat {pid_path} 2>/dev/null) 2>/dev/null; echo $?",
363	                        timeout=5,
364	                    )
365	                    exit_str = exit_result.get("output", "").strip()
366	                    try:
367	                        session.exit_code = int(exit_str.splitlines()[-1].strip())
368	                    except (ValueError, IndexError):
369	                        session.exit_code = -1
370	                    session.exited = True
371	                    self._move_to_finished(session)
372	                    return
373	
374	            except Exception:
375	                # Environment might be gone (sandbox reaped, etc.)
376	                session.exited = True
377	                session.exit_code = -1
378	                self._move_to_finished(session)
379	                return
380	
381	    def _pty_reader_loop(self, session: ProcessSession):
382	        """Background thread: read output from a PTY process."""
383	        pty = session._pty
384	        try:
385	            while pty.isalive():
386	                try:
387	                    chunk = pty.read(4096)
388	                    if chunk:
389	                        # ptyprocess returns bytes
390	                        text = chunk if isinstance(chunk, str) else chunk.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
391	                        with session._lock:
392	                            session.output_buffer += text
393	                            if len(session.output_buffer) > session.max_output_chars:
394	                                session.output_buffer = session.output_buffer[-session.max_output_chars:]
395	                except EOFError:
396	                    break
397	                except Exception:
398	                    break
399	        except Exception as e:
400	            logger.debug("PTY stdout reader ended: %s", e)
401	
402	        # Process exited
403	        try:
404	            pty.wait()
405	        except Exception as e:
406	            logger.debug("PTY wait timed out or failed: %s", e)
407	        session.exited = True
408	        session.exit_code = pty.exitstatus if hasattr(pty, 'exitstatus') else -1
409	        self._move_to_finished(session)
410	
411	    def _move_to_finished(self, session: ProcessSession):
412	        """Move a session from running to finished."""
413	        with self._lock:
414	            self._running.pop(session.id, None)
415	            self._finished[session.id] = session
416	        self._write_checkpoint()
417	
418	    # ----- Query Methods -----
419	
420	    def get(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[ProcessSession]:
421	        """Get a session by ID (running or finished)."""
422	        with self._lock:
423	            return self._running.get(session_id) or self._finished.get(session_id)
424	
425	    def poll(self, session_id: str) -> dict:
426	        """Check status and get new output for a background process."""
427	        from tools.ansi_strip import strip_ansi
428	
429	        session = self.get(session_id)
430	        if session is None:
431	            return {"status": "not_found", "error": f"No process with ID {session_id}"}
432	
433	        with session._lock:
434	            output_preview = strip_ansi(session.output_buffer[-1000:]) if session.output_buffer else ""
435	
436	        result = {
437	            "session_id": session.id,
438	            "command": session.command,
439	            "status": "exited" if session.exited else "running",
440	            "pid": session.pid,
441	            "uptime_seconds": int(time.time() - session.started_at),
442	            "output_preview": output_preview,
443	        }
444	        if session.exited:
445	            result["exit_code"] = session.exit_code
446	        if session.detached:
447	            result["detached"] = True
448	            result["note"] = "Process recovered after restart -- output history unavailable"
449	        return result
450	
451	    def read_log(self, session_id: str, offset: int = 0, limit: int = 200) -> dict:
452	        """Read the full output log with optional pagination by lines."""
453	        from tools.ansi_strip import strip_ansi
454	
455	        session = self.get(session_id)
456	        if session is None:
457	            return {"status": "not_found", "error": f"No process with ID {session_id}"}
458	
459	        with session._lock:
460	            full_output = strip_ansi(session.output_buffer)
461	
462	        lines = full_output.splitlines()
463	        total_lines = len(lines)
464	
465	        # Default: last N lines
466	        if offset == 0 and limit > 0:
467	            selected = lines[-limit:]
468	        else:
469	            selected = lines[offset:offset + limit]
470	
471	        return {
472	            "session_id": session.id,
473	            "status": "exited" if session.exited else "running",
474	            "output": "\n".join(selected),
475	            "total_lines": total_lines,
476	            "showing": f"{len(selected)} lines",
477	        }
478	
479	    def wait(self, session_id: str, timeout: int = None) -> dict:
480	        """
481	        Block until a process exits, timeout, or interrupt.
482	
483	        Args:
484	            session_id: The process to wait for.
485	            timeout: Max seconds to block. Falls back to TERMINAL_TIMEOUT config.
486	
487	        Returns:
488	            dict with status ("exited", "timeout", "interrupted", "not_found")
489	            and output snapshot.
490	        """
491	        from tools.ansi_strip import strip_ansi
492	        from tools.terminal_tool import _interrupt_event
493	
494	        default_timeout = int(os.getenv("TERMINAL_TIMEOUT", "180"))
495	        max_timeout = default_timeout
496	        requested_timeout = timeout
497	        timeout_note = None
498	
499	        if requested_timeout and requested_timeout > max_timeout:
500	            effective_timeout = max_timeout
501	            timeout_note = (
502	                f"Requested wait of {requested_timeout}s was clamped "
503	                f"to configured limit of {max_timeout}s"
504	            )
505	        else:
506	            effective_timeout = requested_timeout or max_timeout
507	
508	        session = self.get(session_id)
509	        if session is None:
510	            return {"status": "not_found", "error": f"No process with ID {session_id}"}
511	
512	        deadline = time.monotonic() + effective_timeout
513	
514	        while time.monotonic() < deadline:
515	            if session.exited:
516	                result = {
517	                    "status": "exited",
518	                    "exit_code": session.exit_code,
519	                    "output": strip_ansi(session.output_buffer[-2000:]),
520	                }
521	                if timeout_note:
522	                    result["timeout_note"] = timeout_note
523	                return result
524	
525	            if _interrupt_event.is_set():
526	                result = {
527	                    "status": "interrupted",
528	                    "output": strip_ansi(session.output_buffer[-1000:]),
529	                    "note": "User sent a new message -- wait interrupted",
530	                }
531	                if timeout_note:
532	                    result["timeout_note"] = timeout_note
533	                return result
534	
535	            time.sleep(1)
536	
537	        result = {
538	            "status": "timeout",
539	            "output": strip_ansi(session.output_buffer[-1000:]),
540	        }
541	        if timeout_note:
542	            result["timeout_note"] = timeout_note
543	        else:
544	            result["timeout_note"] = f"Waited {effective_timeout}s, process still running"
545	        return result
546	
547	    def kill_process(self, session_id: str) -> dict:
548	        """Kill a background process."""
549	        session = self.get(session_id)
550	        if session is None:
551	            return {"status": "not_found", "error": f"No process with ID {session_id}"}
552	
553	        if session.exited:
554	            return {
555	                "status": "already_exited",
556	                "exit_code": session.exit_code,
557	            }
558	
559	        # Kill via PTY, Popen (local), or env execute (non-local)
560	        try:
561	            if session._pty:
562	                # PTY process -- terminate via ptyprocess
563	                try:
564	                    session._pty.terminate(force=True)
565	                except Exception:
566	                    if session.pid:
567	                        os.kill(session.pid, signal.SIGTERM)
568	            elif session.process:
569	                # Local process -- kill the process group
570	                try:
571	                    if _IS_WINDOWS:
572	                        session.process.terminate()
573	                    else:
574	                        os.killpg(os.getpgid(session.process.pid), signal.SIGTERM)
575	                except (ProcessLookupError, PermissionError):
576	                    session.process.kill()
577	            elif session.env_ref and session.pid:
578	                # Non-local -- kill inside sandbox
579	                session.env_ref.execute(f"kill {session.pid} 2>/dev/null", timeout=5)
580	            session.exited = True
581	            session.exit_code = -15  # SIGTERM
582	            self._move_to_finished(session)
583	            self._write_checkpoint()
584	            return {"status": "killed", "session_id": session.id}
585	        except Exception as e:
586	            return {"status": "error", "error": str(e)}
587	
588	    def write_stdin(self, session_id: str, data: str) -> dict:
589	        """Send raw data to a running process's stdin (no newline appended)."""
590	        session = self.get(session_id)
591	        if session is None:
592	            return {"status": "not_found", "error": f"No process with ID {session_id}"}
593	        if session.exited:
594	            return {"status": "already_exited", "error": "Process has already finished"}
595	
596	        # PTY mode -- write through pty handle (expects bytes)
597	        if hasattr(session, '_pty') and session._pty:
598	            try:
599	                pty_data = data.encode("utf-8") if isinstance(data, str) else data
600	                session._pty.write(pty_data)
601	                return {"status": "ok", "bytes_written": len(data)}
602	            except Exception as e:
603	                return {"status": "error", "error": str(e)}
604	
605	        # Popen mode -- write through stdin pipe
606	        if not session.process or not session.process.stdin:
607	            return {"status": "error", "error": "Process stdin not available (non-local backend or stdin closed)"}
608	        try:
609	            session.process.stdin.write(data)
610	            session.process.stdin.flush()
611	            return {"status": "ok", "bytes_written": len(data)}
612	        except Exception as e:
613	            return {"status": "error", "error": str(e)}
614	
615	    def submit_stdin(self, session_id: str, data: str = "") -> dict:
616	        """Send data + newline to a running process's stdin (like pressing Enter)."""
617	        return self.write_stdin(session_id, data + "\n")
618	
619	    def list_sessions(self, task_id: str = None) -> list:
620	        """List all running and recently-finished processes."""
621	        with self._lock:
622	            all_sessions = list(self._running.values()) + list(self._finished.values())
623	
624	        if task_id:
625	            all_sessions = [s for s in all_sessions if s.task_id == task_id]
626	
627	        result = []
628	        for s in all_sessions:
629	            entry = {
630	                "session_id": s.id,
631	                "command": s.command[:200],
632	                "cwd": s.cwd,
633	                "pid": s.pid,
634	                "started_at": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S", time.localtime(s.started_at)),
635	                "uptime_seconds": int(time.time() - s.started_at),
636	                "status": "exited" if s.exited else "running",
637	                "output_preview": s.output_buffer[-200:] if s.output_buffer else "",
638	            }
639	            if s.exited:
640	                entry["exit_code"] = s.exit_code
641	            if s.detached:
642	                entry["detached"] = True
643	            result.append(entry)
644	        return result
645	
646	    # ----- Session/Task Queries (for gateway integration) -----
647	
648	    def has_active_processes(self, task_id: str) -> bool:
649	        """Check if there are active (running) processes for a task_id."""
650	        with self._lock:
651	            return any(
652	                s.task_id == task_id and not s.exited
653	                for s in self._running.values()
654	            )
655	
656	    def has_active_for_session(self, session_key: str) -> bool:
657	        """Check if there are active processes for a gateway session key."""
658	        with self._lock:
659	            return any(
660	                s.session_key == session_key and not s.exited
661	                for s in self._running.values()
662	            )
663	
664	    def kill_all(self, task_id: str = None) -> int:
665	        """Kill all running processes, optionally filtered by task_id. Returns count killed."""
666	        with self._lock:
667	            targets = [
668	                s for s in self._running.values()
669	                if (task_id is None or s.task_id == task_id) and not s.exited
670	            ]
671	
672	        killed = 0
673	        for session in targets:
674	            result = self.kill_process(session.id)
675	            if result.get("status") in ("killed", "already_exited"):
676	                killed += 1
677	        return killed
678	
679	    # ----- Cleanup / Pruning -----
680	
681	    def _prune_if_needed(self):
682	        """Remove oldest finished sessions if over MAX_PROCESSES. Must hold _lock."""
683	        # First prune expired finished sessions
684	        now = time.time()
685	        expired = [
686	            sid for sid, s in self._finished.items()
687	            if (now - s.started_at) > FINISHED_TTL_SECONDS
688	        ]
689	        for sid in expired:
690	            del self._finished[sid]
691	
692	        # If still over limit, remove oldest finished
693	        total = len(self._running) + len(self._finished)
694	        if total >= MAX_PROCESSES and self._finished:
695	            oldest_id = min(self._finished, key=lambda sid: self._finished[sid].started_at)
696	            del self._finished[oldest_id]
697	
698	    def cleanup_expired(self):
699	        """Public method to prune expired finished sessions."""
700	        with self._lock:
701	            self._prune_if_needed()
702	
703	    # ----- Checkpoint (crash recovery) -----
704	
705	    def _write_checkpoint(self):
706	        """Write running process metadata to checkpoint file atomically."""
707	        try:
708	            with self._lock:
709	                entries = []
710	                for s in self._running.values():
711	                    if not s.exited:
712	                        entries.append({
713	                            "session_id": s.id,
714	                            "command": s.command,
715	                            "pid": s.pid,
716	                            "cwd": s.cwd,
717	                            "started_at": s.started_at,
718	                            "task_id": s.task_id,
719	                            "session_key": s.session_key,
720	                            "watcher_platform": s.watcher_platform,
721	                            "watcher_chat_id": s.watcher_chat_id,
722	                            "watcher_thread_id": s.watcher_thread_id,
723	                            "watcher_interval": s.watcher_interval,
724	                        })
725	            
726	            # Atomic write to avoid corruption on crash
727	            from utils import atomic_json_write
728	            atomic_json_write(CHECKPOINT_PATH, entries)
729	        except Exception as e:
730	            logger.debug("Failed to write checkpoint file: %s", e, exc_info=True)
731	
732	    def recover_from_checkpoint(self) -> int:
733	        """
734	        On gateway startup, probe PIDs from checkpoint file.
735	
736	        Returns the number of processes recovered as detached.
737	        """
738	        if not CHECKPOINT_PATH.exists():
739	            return 0
740	
741	        try:
742	            entries = json.loads(CHECKPOINT_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
743	        except Exception:
744	            return 0
745	
746	        recovered = 0
747	        for entry in entries:
748	            pid = entry.get("pid")
749	            if not pid:
750	                continue
751	
752	            # Check if PID is still alive
753	            alive = False
754	            try:
755	                os.kill(pid, 0)
756	                alive = True
757	            except (ProcessLookupError, PermissionError):
758	                pass
759	
760	            if alive:
761	                session = ProcessSession(
762	                    id=entry["session_id"],
763	                    command=entry.get("command", "unknown"),
764	                    task_id=entry.get("task_id", ""),
765	                    session_key=entry.get("session_key", ""),
766	                    pid=pid,
767	                    cwd=entry.get("cwd"),
768	                    started_at=entry.get("started_at", time.time()),
769	                    detached=True,  # Can't read output, but can report status + kill
770	                    watcher_platform=entry.get("watcher_platform", ""),
771	                    watcher_chat_id=entry.get("watcher_chat_id", ""),
772	                    watcher_thread_id=entry.get("watcher_thread_id", ""),
773	                    watcher_interval=entry.get("watcher_interval", 0),
774	                )
775	                with self._lock:
776	                    self._running[session.id] = session
777	                recovered += 1
778	                logger.info("Recovered detached process: %s (pid=%d)", session.command[:60], pid)
779	
780	                # Re-enqueue watcher so gateway can resume notifications
781	                if session.watcher_interval > 0:
782	                    self.pending_watchers.append({
783	                        "session_id": session.id,
784	                        "check_interval": session.watcher_interval,
785	                        "session_key": session.session_key,
786	                        "platform": session.watcher_platform,
787	                        "chat_id": session.watcher_chat_id,
788	                        "thread_id": session.watcher_thread_id,
789	                    })
790	
791	        # Clear the checkpoint (will be rewritten as processes finish)
792	        try:
793	            from utils import atomic_json_write
794	            atomic_json_write(CHECKPOINT_PATH, [])
795	        except Exception as e:
796	            logger.debug("Could not clear checkpoint file: %s", e, exc_info=True)
797	
798	        return recovered
799	
800	
801	# Module-level singleton
802	process_registry = ProcessRegistry()
803	
804	
805	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
806	# Registry -- the "process" tool schema + handler
807	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
808	from tools.registry import registry
809	
810	PROCESS_SCHEMA = {
811	    "name": "process",
812	    "description": (
813	        "Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). "
814	        "Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), "
815	        "'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), "
816	        "'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), "
817	        "'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts)."
818	    ),
819	    "parameters": {
820	        "type": "object",
821	        "properties": {
822	            "action": {
823	                "type": "string",
824	                "enum": ["list", "poll", "log", "wait", "kill", "write", "submit"],
825	                "description": "Action to perform on background processes"
826	            },
827	            "session_id": {
828	                "type": "string",
829	                "description": "Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'."
830	            },
831	            "data": {
832	                "type": "string",
833	                "description": "Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)"
834	            },
835	            "timeout": {
836	                "type": "integer",
837	                "description": "Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.",
838	                "minimum": 1
839	            },
840	            "offset": {
841	                "type": "integer",
842	                "description": "Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)"
843	            },
844	            "limit": {
845	                "type": "integer",
846	                "description": "Max lines to return for 'log' action",
847	                "minimum": 1
848	            }
849	        },
850	        "required": ["action"]
851	    }
852	}
853	
854	
855	def _handle_process(args, **kw):
856	    import json as _json
857	    task_id = kw.get("task_id")
858	    action = args.get("action", "")
859	    # Coerce to string — some models send session_id as an integer
860	    session_id = str(args.get("session_id", "")) if args.get("session_id") is not None else ""
861	
862	    if action == "list":
863	        return _json.dumps({"processes": process_registry.list_sessions(task_id=task_id)}, ensure_ascii=False)
864	    elif action in ("poll", "log", "wait", "kill", "write", "submit"):
865	        if not session_id:
866	            return _json.dumps({"error": f"session_id is required for {action}"}, ensure_ascii=False)
867	        if action == "poll":
868	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.poll(session_id), ensure_ascii=False)
869	        elif action == "log":
870	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.read_log(
871	                session_id, offset=args.get("offset", 0), limit=args.get("limit", 200)), ensure_ascii=False)
872	        elif action == "wait":
873	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.wait(session_id, timeout=args.get("timeout")), ensure_ascii=False)
874	        elif action == "kill":
875	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.kill_process(session_id), ensure_ascii=False)
876	        elif action == "write":
877	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.write_stdin(session_id, str(args.get("data", ""))), ensure_ascii=False)
878	        elif action == "submit":
879	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.submit_stdin(session_id, str(args.get("data", ""))), ensure_ascii=False)
880	    return _json.dumps({"error": f"Unknown process action: {action}. Use: list, poll, log, wait, kill, write, submit"}, ensure_ascii=False)
881	
882	
883	registry.register(
884	    name="process",
885	    toolset="terminal",
886	    schema=PROCESS_SCHEMA,
887	    handler=_handle_process,
888	    emoji="⚙️",
889	)
890
